headline: HSBC charged for helping former Lebanese central bank head embezzle hundreds of millions
description: The Swiss subsidiary of HSBC is suspected of having helped former Lebanese central bank chief Riad Salameh embezzle more than $300 million from the country's central bank. The charges mark a significant development in the French judicial investigation.

Hungary's Magyar to Le Monde: 'I was not elected simply to change the government, but to change the regime'
France outraged as suspect in missing girl case had prior rape complaints
description: Dozens of police and volunteers have been searching the southwestern French countryside for days for 11-year-old Lyhanna, as criticism grows after it emerged that the main suspect had previously been accused of sexual violence.

headline: Days after heatwave, French government cuts climate change adaptation funds
description: The Green Fund, which helps local authorities finance environmental protection and climate adaptation projects, is facing a €162.5 million cut, a nearly 20% reduction.

headline: Andreeva of Russia beats Kostyuk of Ukraine to reach French Open final
description: Russian teenager Mirra Andreeva reached her first Grand Slam final by beating Marta Kostyuk 6-1, 6-3 at the French Open on Thursday.

headline: Thomas Piketty's lab proposes ambitious plan to cut global inequality and curb climate change
description: Building a more equitable world while respecting planetary limits is possible, economists from the World Inequality Lab assert in a report published Thursday. To achieve this, they say, targeted reductions in consumption in specific sectors, a drastic cut in fossil fuel use and changes in dietary habits will be necessary.

Marjane Satrapi: 'At age 10, I was training myself to become a political prisoner'
description: The Franco-Iranian artist, who gained worldwide recognition with the graphic novel 'Persepolis,' died at 56 years old, her family announced Thursday. Le Monde interviewed her in October 2020. We are republishing that interview today.

headline: Who is rich in France?
description: Is a childless couple wealthy if they earn €6,438 per month after taxes? A new report seeks to address the lack of set criteria for measuring this demographic.

headline: The 'palace' paradox: Most of France's top luxury hotels are owned by foreigners
description: The government's latest 'Palaces de France' list was revealed on June 2, with six new additions and four removals. The label aims to showcase this flagship sector of French expertise, even though the establishments' owners are rarely French.
